steps.title: Backend: GET /api/jobs/:id already exists — verify it works
manual_steps: With backend running, open browser to http://localhost:8080/api/jobs
Copy any job's id field from the JSON response
Visit http://localhost:8080/api/jobs/THAT_ID — should return the single job object
If you see the job JSON, proceed to 2.2

steps.title: Add getJobById to api.js + add route to App.jsx
steps.prompt:
Make two small changes:

1. In frontend/src/services/api.js, add this export:
export const getJobById = (id) => api.get(`/jobs/${id}`);

2. In frontend/src/App.jsx:
- Import JobDetail from './pages/JobDetail' (we'll create it next)
- Add a route: } />

steps.title: Create JobDetail.jsx page
Wire JobCard onClick to navigate to detail page
steps.prompt:
Update JobCard.jsx:
- Import useNavigate from react-router-dom
- Inside the component, call const navigate = useNavigate();
- The onClick prop on the title and the "View Details" button should call: navigate(`/jobs/${job.id}`)
- Remove the onClick prop from Jobs.jsx (it's no longer needed as a prop)

steps.title: Create FilterSidebar.jsx component
Wire FilterSidebar into Jobs.jsx
steps.prompt:
Update Jobs.jsx to wire up the FilterSidebar:

1. Import FilterSidebar from ../components/FilterSidebar
2. Add state: const [filters, setFilters] = useState({ types: [], categories: [], locations: [] })
3. Add a handleFilterChange function:
   - If filterType === 'clear': setFilters({ types:[], categories:[], locations:[] })
   - Otherwise: toggle the value in the array — if checked, add it; if unchecked, remove it

4. Add a filteredJobs computed value (useMemo or just inline):
   Filter jobs array where:
   - If filters.types.length > 0: job.type must be in filters.types
   - If filters.categories.length > 0: job.category must be in filters.categories
   - If filters.locations.length > 0: job.location must be in filters.locations
   All active filters must match (AND logic)

5. Replace the placeholder filters-sidebar div with 
6. Map over filteredJobs instead of jobs in the render
7. Update the count to show filteredJobs.length

steps.title: Backend: Create Application model
steps.prompt:
Create backend/src/main/java/com/thrall/backend/model/Application.java

@Document(collection = "applications")
@Data @NoArgsConstructor @AllArgsConstructor from Lombok

Fields:
- String id (@Id)
- String jobId
- String userId
- String applicantName
- String applicantEmail
- String phone
- String coverLetter
- String status (default "Applied") — values: "Applied", "Viewed", "Shortlisted", "Rejected"
- LocalDateTime appliedAt (= LocalDateTime.now())

steps.title: Backend: ApplicationRepository + ApplicationController
steps.prompt:
Create two files in the backend:

FILE 1: repository/ApplicationRepository.java
Interface extending MongoRepository
Custom methods:
- List findByUserId(String userId)
- List findByJobId(String jobId)
- boolean existsByJobIdAndUserId(String jobId, String userId)

FILE 2: controller/ApplicationController.java
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/applications") @CrossOrigin(origins="http://localhost:5173")
Inject ApplicationRepository.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/applications
  Body: Application object
  Check if user already applied: if (applicationRepository.existsByJobIdAndUserId(body.getJobId(), body.getUserId())) return 409 with message "Already applied"
  Otherwise save and return 201

- GET /api/applications/user/{userId}
  Return all applications by this user (for My Applications page)

- GET /api/applications/job/{jobId}
  Return all applications for a job (for employer view later)

steps.title: Frontend: Add applyToJob to api.js + create ApplyPage.jsx
Create Profile.jsx page
Update Navbar to show Profile link when logged in
steps.prompt:
Update Navbar.jsx's logged-in state:
Replace the plain "Welcome, {name}" span with a dropdown-style link:
- Show a clickable avatar circle (28px, white letter on green background) + the user's first name next to it
- Clicking it navigates to /profile using a Link tag
- Keep the Logout button next to it
Also add a "My Applications" Link to /profile in the nav when user is logged in (show it as a nav-link item before the avatar)

steps.title: Backend: addJobPostedBy field to Job model
steps.prompt:
Add one field to Job.java:
private String postedByUserId;   // userId of the person who posted this job

This is optional (can be null for seeded jobs). No other changes needed — Spring Data MongoDB will handle it automatically.

steps.title: Create PostJob.jsx page
Update CategoryGrid to navigate to filtered jobs
steps.prompt:
Rewrite CategoryGrid.jsx:

Import useNavigate. Each category card, when clicked, calls:
navigate('/jobs', { state: { filterCategory: categoryName } })

Update the category list with icons (use emoji) and real counts:
const categories = [
  { name: 'IT & Software', icon: '💻', count: 8 },
  { name: 'Marketing', icon: '📢', count: 3 },
  { name: 'Design', icon: '🎨', count: 2 },
  { name: 'Finance', icon: '💰', count: 2 },
  { name: 'Engineering', icon: '⚙️', count: 2 },
  { name: 'Sales', icon: '🤝', count: 2 },
  { name: 'Management', icon: '📋', count: 1 },
  { name: 'Operations', icon: '🔧', count: 1 },
]

Each card shows: icon (large), category name, count as "X jobs"

Update .category-card CSS in index.css:
.category-card { cursor:pointer; transition:transform 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s; }
.category-card:hover { transform:translateY(-3px); box-shadow:0 6px 20px rgba(64,130,109,0.15); border-color:var(--primary-color); }

steps.title: Handle filterCategory state in Jobs.jsx
steps.prompt:
In Jobs.jsx, check for location.state?.filterCategory on mount.
If it exists, pre-set the filters state to: { types:[], categories:[filterCategory], locations:[] }
This way clicking a home page category card pre-filters the jobs list automatically.
Add this inside the useEffect that already checks for searchResults.

steps.title: Update Hero section — add job count stat + better CTA
steps.prompt:
Rewrite Hero.jsx:

- Import getAllJobs from ../services/api and useEffect, useState
- On mount, fetch all jobs and store count in state (jobCount)
- Display: "Find Your Dream Job" as the main heading
- Subheading: "Join {jobCount || '25,000+'} job seekers already using Thrall to land their dream role"
- Three stat pills below the description in a flex row:
    "25+ Live Jobs", "10+ Companies", "Free for Job Seekers"
- Change "Browse Internships" button to a React Router Link to /jobs with state { filterCategory: 'IT & Software' } text "Browse IT Jobs"
- "Search Jobs" anchor stays pointing to #search

CSS to add:
.hero-stats { display:flex; gap:20px; justify-content:center; margin-top:20px; flex-wrap:wrap; }
.hero-stat { background:rgba(255,255,255,0.15); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.3); border-radius:20px; padding:6px 16px; font-size:13px; font-weight:600; }

steps.title: Add "Already Applied" badge on JobCard when user is logged in
steps.prompt:
This adds a "✓ Applied" indicator on job cards the user has already applied to.

In Jobs.jsx:
- Import getMyApplications from ../services/api
- On mount, if user is logged in (check localStorage), fetch getMyApplications(user.id) and store result in state as myApplications (array)
- Compute a Set: const appliedJobIds = new Set(myApplications.map(a => a.jobId))
- Pass applied={appliedJobIds.has(job.id)} prop to each JobCard

In JobCard.jsx:
- Accept new prop: applied (boolean)
- In the job-card-footer, if applied is true, show a green "✓ Applied" badge (styled with the .status-applied CSS) instead of (or next to) the "View Details" button

CSS to add:
.applied-badge { font-size:12px; font-weight:700; color:#40826D; background:#e8f5f1; border:1px solid #40826D; border-radius:20px; padding:4px 12px; }

steps.title: Final end-to-end test checklist
steps.prompt:
cd "/Users/himishavyas/Documents/Code Files/Full Stack Lab/React/JobQuest/backend"
./mvnw spring-boot:run
manual_steps: Homepage loads — hero shows live job count, category cards visible
Click a category card → /jobs pre-filtered by that category
Sidebar filters work — check Full-time + Bangalore → fewer results
Click a job card → /jobs/:id loads full detail page
Register a new account → login → name + avatar shows in navbar
Click "Apply Now" → fill form → submit → success message appears
Go back to /jobs → that job card shows "✓ Applied" badge
Click "My Applications" in navbar → see the application with "Applied" status badge
Click "Post a Job" → fill form → submit → new job appears in /jobs

- frontend runs on :5173, backend on :8080
- all CORS is handled with @CrossOrigin(origins = "http://localhost:5173")
- DB name: jobquestdb
